common_name: Red Dragon Fruit (Costa Rica Pitaya, Red-Flesh Pitahaya)

planting.note: Root stem cuttings 12–18 inches long in well-drained sandy mix in warm conditions; red dragon fruit requires cross-pollination from a different plant or variety — hand-pollinate large night-blooming flowers with a brush.

directions: Root stem cuttings in gritty, well-drained cactus mix and grow in full sun with a sturdy trellis or post for the sprawling succulent stems to climb; red-flesh dragon fruit (Selenicereus costaricensis) is distinguished from the common white-flesh variety by its dramatically vivid magenta-red flesh with a sweeter, more pronounced flavor and significantly higher antioxidant content (including betalains that turn urine temporarily pink). The spectacular dinner-plate-sized white flowers open for only one night and must be hand-pollinated in gardens outside the tropics. Fruits follow 30–50 days after successful pollination and ripen to striking deep red skin. Drought-tolerant once established; overwatering is the most common cause of decline.

common_name: Yellow Dragon Fruit (Yellow Pitahaya, Cactus Pear Pitaya)

planting.note: Root stem cuttings in well-drained cactus mix in warm conditions; yellow pitahaya is self-fertile — a single plant will set fruit without hand pollination, unlike most other dragon fruit species.

directions: Root stem cuttings in gritty, well-drained cactus mix and grow on a strong post or trellis in full sun; yellow pitahaya is considered by many tasters to be the sweetest and most flavorful dragon fruit — its white interior flesh with tiny edible black seeds is measurably higher in sugar than red or white-flesh types, with a rich, tropical, almost honey-like sweetness and no bitterness. The fruits have yellow, spiny (not scaly) skin without the prominent pink 'scales' of other types. A significant advantage over other dragon fruits: yellow pitahaya is self-fertile and does not require hand pollination or a second plant to set abundant crops. Grows slower than Selenicereus undatus but the superior flavor makes it the most prized of the dragon fruit species.
